2009-03-01.title: Legal Battles Await Olmert as Term Ends
2009-03-01.abstract: Coaxing a central witness back for more testimony takes on urgency as prosecutors struggle to complete their corruption investigation of Israeli Prime Minister Ehud Olmert.

2009-03-01.title: For Zimbabwe, Party Is a Chance to Eat, Not Cheer
2009-03-01.abstract: President Robert Mugabe spent $250,000 on his birthday party while millions of people in Zimbabwe need emergency food aid to stave off starvation.

2009-03-01.title: Rhena Schweitzer Miller, 90, Dies; Aided Father’s Work
2009-03-01.abstract: Mrs. Miller was the child of the Nobel laureate Dr. Albert Schweitzer and the director of the hospital that her father and mother opened in a rain forest in west central Africa.

2009-03-01.title: Families Search for Truth of Spain’s ‘Lost Children’
2009-03-01.abstract: Provincial judges are investigating the cases of children taken from left-wing families as part of an effort to purge Franco’s Spain of Marxist influence.

2009-03-01.title: Obama’s Backing Raises Hopes for Climate Pact
2009-03-01.abstract: The perception that the U.S. is now serious about tackling climate change has set off a flurry of diplomacy around the globe.

2009-03-02.title: Ukraine Teeters as Citizens Blame Banks and Government
2009-03-02.abstract: Factories and services are faltering, the currency is wilting and a government default seems possible, posing a real threat to other European economies.

2009-03-02.title: For Some Taxi Drivers, a Different Kind of Traffic
2009-03-02.abstract: With public outrage over drug violence tinged by fascination, some taxi drivers are giving “narco-tours” of sites associated with the notorious drug cartels.

2009-03-02.title: Facing Counterfeiting Crackdown, Beijing Vendors Fight Back
2009-03-02.abstract: Tenacious vendors have staged protests against lawyers who are pursuing trademark cases, mocking them as bourgeois puppets of foreigners.

2009-03-02.title: Clinton Heads to Israel and West Bank Bearing Cash and Caution
2009-03-02.abstract: In her first foray into Middle East diplomacy, Hillary Rodham Clinton arrived in the region with a pledge of $300 million for war-torn Gaza.

2009-03-02.title: Radio Reporter From the U.S. Is Held by Iran
2009-03-02.abstract: Roxana Saberi, an Iranian-American reporter who worked as a freelancer in Iran for six years, was arrested after she bought a bottle of wine.

2009-03-03.title: Castro, With Entourage, Is Spotted on Strolls
2009-03-03.abstract: Witnesses said they spotted the Cuban leader taking an early morning walk through an exclusive neighborhood on the outskirts of Havana.

2009-03-03.title: Melting Pot of Melodrama Enthralls French Nightly
2009-03-03.abstract: Some 13 million people, nearly a fifth of the French population, tune in weekly to follow the convoluted fates of the families on the soap opera “Plus Belle la Vie.”

2009-03-03.title: For Saudi Liberals, a Ripple of Hope in a Sea of Tradition
2009-03-03.abstract: Many have hailed King Abdullah’s recent cabinet reshuffle as a “mini-revolution” and proof that he is at last willing to tame the country’s religious establishment.

2009-03-03.title: Grass-Roots Uprising Against River Dam Challenges Tokyo
2009-03-03.abstract: Uprisings have cropped up in Japan ever since a small citizen group blocked the government from building a dam.

2009-03-03.title: Vatican: Pope Withdraws Promotion of Priest
2009-03-03.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI withdrew the promotion of a priest who had said that Hurricane Katrina was God’s punishment of New Orleanians and that homosexuality was curable.

2009-03-04.title: Reporter's Notebook: A Fragile Optimism
2009-03-04.abstract: The mountain gorillas in Eastern Congo stand to benefit from the recent departure of rebels under General Laurent Nkunda.

2009-03-04.title: In Congo, With Rebels Now at Bay, Calm Erupts
2009-03-04.abstract: If the endangered mountain gorillas are any sign, things may finally be looking up in eastern Congo as Rwandan aid in combating rebels has paid off.

2009-03-04.title: Avoiding Pakistan, New Supply Route to Afghanistan Opens
2009-03-04.abstract: A new route through Uzbekistan provides an alternative to the one through the troubled border with Pakistan.

2009-03-04.title: Economic Woes in Latvia Fuel Public’s Anger at Government
2009-03-04.abstract: Demonstrations by dairy farmers and others suffering from financial distress are focusing outrage, but straining the former Soviet republic.

2009-03-04.title: Amid a Lackluster Review of His First Year, Cuba’s Leader Jolts the Government
2009-03-04.abstract: As he ended his first year as president, Raúl Castro ousted several of the government’s most familiar officials, including the foreign minister.

2009-03-05.title: 50 Years After Revolt, Clampdown on Tibetans
2009-03-05.abstract: Chinese authorities have imposed unofficial martial law in areas where ethnic Tibetans live.

2009-03-05.title: Another Karzai Forges Afghan Business Empire
2009-03-05.abstract: Mahmoud Karzai’s swift rise has stirred suspicion among Afghans, who resent his brother’s seeming tolerance for unsavory activities.

2009-03-05.title: Bill in Senate Spurs Debate Over Easing Ban on Cuba
2009-03-05.abstract: The $410 billion spending bill working its way through Congress would ease some travel and trade restrictions enacted under the Bush administration.

2009-03-05.title: Zimbabwe: Opposition Figure Still Jailed Despite Bail Ruling
2009-03-05.abstract: The Movement for Democratic Change said that prison officials had said that appointees of President Robert Mugabe were blocking the release of an opposition figure.

2009-03-05.title: China: Bankrupt Purveyor of Contaminated Milk Is Sold
2009-03-05.abstract: The Sanlu Group was bought at auction on Wednesday for $90.3 million by the Beijing Sanyuan Group Company.

2009-03-06.title: In South Korea, Drinks Are on the Maple Tree
2009-03-06.abstract: For centuries, southern Korean villagers have been drinking the sap of the gorosoe, or “tree good for the bones.”

2009-03-06.title: New Trial for Tycoon Is a Test for Russia
2009-03-06.abstract: After more than five years in prison, Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky appears ready to act on what may be a possibility for negotiations with the Kremlin to secure his freedom.

2009-03-06.title: Germany’s Leader in Hot Seat Over G.M. Aid Request
2009-03-06.abstract: Whether Angela Merkel will grant public financing to Opel, a storied German brand that G.M. bought 80 years ago, has become a highly politicized question.

2009-03-06.title: Truce in Pakistan May Mean Leeway for Taliban
2009-03-06.abstract: An accord between militants and the Pakistani Army seems to have allowed the Taliban free rein in Swat.

2009-03-06.title: China Calls for Closer Ties With Taiwan
2009-03-06.abstract: Prime Minister Wen Jiabao signaled support for the gradual integration of banking and financial services across the Taiwan Straits.

2009-03-07.title: 6 Years In, Troops Glimpse Real Path Out of Iraq
2009-03-07.abstract: In many parts of Iraq, the contours of an American exit strategy have taken clearer shape than at any time before.

2009-03-07.title: An Empire for Poor Working Women, Guided by a Gandhian Approach
2009-03-07.abstract: Ela Bhatt, whose Self-Employed Women’s Association offers a safety net to poor working women, is a Gandhian pragmatist for the New India.

2009-03-07.title: Illness Strikes Patrons of Fat Duck Restaurant in Britain
2009-03-07.abstract: The Fat Duck restaurant is closed as investigators conduct an array of tests to find out why hundreds of diners reported becoming violently sick.

2009-03-07.title: Desert’s Sand and Rocks Become Precious Resources in West Bank Dispute
2009-03-07.abstract: A legal challenge by an Israeli rights group objects to Israel’s quarrying in the West Bank and profiting from it.

2009-03-07.title: Officer Leads Old Corps in New Role in Pakistan
2009-03-07.abstract: The Frontier Corps, an oft-maligned outfit led by Maj. Gen. Tariq Khan, is emerging on the front lines against a rapidly spreading insurgency in Pakistan.

2009-03-08.title: Confronting Rape on Zimbabwe's Border
2009-03-08.abstract: As women flee from Zimbabwe, waiting for some are gumagumas, men who offer themselves as guides but are actually rapists.

2009-03-08.title: Drifting in the Desert
2009-03-08.abstract: Robert Worth reports on car culture from the streets of Saudi Arabia.

2009-03-08.title: Correction
2009-03-08.abstract: An article last Sunday about military consultations between China and the United States in Beijing misstated the title of the Defense Department official who led the American delegation. The official, David Sedney, is the deputy assistant secretary of defense for East Asia. (There is no under secretary of defense for East Asia.).

2009-03-08.title: Suicide Attack Kills 28 in Baghdad
2009-03-08.abstract: At least one suicide bomber blew himself up in the busy early morning hours on Sunday near the police academy in Baghdad.

2009-03-08.title: In North Korean Poll, Observers Look Down Ballot
2009-03-08.abstract: North Korea elected its rubber-stamp parliament on Sunday in a poll that outside observers monitored closely for signs of a shift in the regime’s power structure.

2009-03-09.title: As Olmert’s Time in Office Nears End, Captive Soldier’s Saga Gains Urgency
2009-03-09.abstract: The parents and friends of an Israeli soldier captured by Hamas are pressing Prime Minister Ehud Olmert to broker a deal for his return.

2009-03-09.title: Inquiry Planned for Cricket Attack
2009-03-09.abstract: After making a number of arrests, the police said a full report on the attack, which killed six officers and a driver, would be presented Friday, but no new details were revealed.

2009-03-09.title: Amid a Vote, North Korea Awaits Clues to Its Future
2009-03-09.abstract: With the election of delegates to the Supreme People’s Assembly, North Korea laid the groundwork for a new five-year term for its leader, Kim Jong-il.

2009-03-09.title: Seeking Justice, Chinese Land in Secret Jails
2009-03-09.abstract: Rights advocates say leaders are using “black houses” to reduce the number of petitioners reaching Beijing.

2009-03-09.title: Correction
2009-03-09.abstract: An article on Feb. 27 about prison sentences handed down by a United Nations court for five senior officials in the government of the former Serbian strongman Slobodan Milosevic misstated the name of the court. It is the International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia, not the United Nations War Crimes Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia.

2009-03-10.title: 2 West Africa Slayings May Signal a New Day
2009-03-10.abstract: The president and army chief of staff of Guinea-Bissau died after a rivalry that has destabilized the country.

2009-03-10.title: Pakistani Tribe Signs Pact to Cooperate With Officials
2009-03-10.abstract: The tribe, which has close ties to the Taliban, agreed to hand over several of the militant group’s local leaders, to lay down their arms and to stop harboring foreign militants.

2009-03-10.title: Trials in Cambodia Expose the Cogs in a Killing Machine
2009-03-10.abstract: The trials of senior Khmer Rouge figures raise questions about the guilt of lower-ranking cadres who say they carried out the regime’s killings under the threat of death.

2009-03-10.title: Swiss Romeo Admits Guilt in Beguiling BMW Heiress
2009-03-10.abstract: A man who admitted wooing, swindling and trying to blackmail Germany’s richest woman was sentenced to six years in prison.

2009-03-10.title: U.S. Halted Some Raids in Afghanistan
2009-03-10.abstract: The halt came after a series of nighttime raids in recent months killed women and children.

2009-03-11.title: Europeans Debate Castration of Sex Offenders
2009-03-11.abstract: Whether castration can help rehabilitate violent sex offenders is under scrutiny in the Czech Republic.

2009-03-11.title: Nominee Ends Bid for Key Job in Intelligence
2009-03-11.abstract: Charles W. Freeman Jr., the Obama administration’s choice for a major intelligence post, says pro-Israel lobbyists had distorted his record and campaigned against him.

2009-03-11.title: Madagascar: Army’s Leader Sets a Deadline
2009-03-11.abstract: Gen. Edmond Rasolomahandry warned the country’s political leaders on Tuesday that the military would “take a crucial decision” if an agreement was not reached within 72 hours.

2009-03-11.title: Afghanistan: Journalist Is Killed
2009-03-11.abstract: An Afghan journalist who had once been detained by the United States was killed Tuesday in a drive-by shooting in Kandahar, Canadian television reported.

2009-03-11.title: Iran: Ex-Premier Announces Candidacy
2009-03-11.abstract: A former prime minister of Iran, Mir Hussein Moussavi, announced Tuesday that he would run in the June presidential race, the Iranian Student News Agency reported.

2009-03-12.title: Elections in El Salvador Invoke Rivalries of Civil War Years
2009-03-12.abstract: The vicious campaigning between the right-wing Nationalist Republican Alliance and the left-wing F.M.L.N. stems from the country’s 12-year civil war, when the two parties were on opposite ends of the conflict.

2009-03-12.title: Congo: Delays in Rebel Leader’s Case
2009-03-12.abstract: Negotiations over the possible extradition of the rebel leader Laurent Nkunda from Rwanda to Congo have been delayed, according to an assistant to the minister of foreign affairs for Rwanda.

2009-03-12.title: Mexico: Drug Lord on Wealth List
2009-03-12.abstract: Forbes’s list of the richest people in the world includes a fugitive drug don from Mexico who goes by the name Shorty.

2009-03-12.title: Georgia Withdraws From Music Contest
2009-03-12.abstract: Contest organizers refused to allow a Georgian pop group to perform a song that pokes fun at Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin of Russia.

2009-03-12.title: Argentina: Inquiry in 1994 Bombing
2009-03-12.abstract: A judge began investigating the possible kidnapping and torture of a lawyer who had alleged a cover-up in a long-running inquiry into the 1994 bombing of a Jewish community center in Buenos Aires.

2009-03-13.title: Tibet Atrocities Dot Official China History
2009-03-13.abstract: A Chinese exhibit is a propagandist tour de force that reinforces the Communist Party’s unbending version of history during what is referred to as a “sensitive time.”

2009-03-13.abstract: A prohibition on ethnic restaurants in Lucca’s historical center has set off a tug of war between the romantic Italy of the popular imagination and the more complex reality.

2009-03-13.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI admitted that the Vatican had made “mistakes” in handling the case of a Holocaust-denying bishop, including not consulting information available online.

2009-03-13.abstract: The Obama administration was somewhat blindsided by Britain’s announcement that it was talking with the militant group’s political wing, an American official said.

2009-03-13.title: Elder Karzai Defends Ties to Business
2009-03-13.abstract: The older brother of President Hamid Karzai said an article in The New York Times about his business activities was an unfounded pre-election smear tactic.

2009-03-14.title: To Make Female Hearts Flutter in Iraq, Throw a Shoe
2009-03-14.abstract: In parks and dress shops, in university halls and on picnics, Iraqi women are still smitten by the show thrower, Muntader al-Zaidi.

2009-03-14.abstract: At annual Communist Party meetings, officials shower superiors with Gucci bags and Swiss watches.

2009-03-14.title: Bangladeshi Premier Faces a Grim Crucible
2009-03-14.abstract: After a February mutiny by border guards, Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina is at odds with the military while she tries to keep the country stable.

2009-03-14.abstract: The peaceful response to a killing in Northern Ireland seems to suggest the province is united against a return of violence.

2009-03-14.abstract: President Cristina Fernández de Kirchner of Argentina said she would ask her country’s Congress to move up elections by four months.

2009-03-15.abstract: After a weekend of protests, the Pakistani government agreed to reinstate the former chief justice of the Supreme Court, a stunning concession to the opposition leader Nawaz Sharif.

2009-03-15.abstract: Some countries have begun raising questions about the risks and requirements if they accept prisoners.

2009-03-15.abstract: The soldiers were killed in a roadside explosion on Sunday, the United States military said, in the worst of several attacks by insurgents around the country.

2009-03-16.abstract: On his first trip to the continent as pontiff, Pope Benedict XVI will encounter a land where the church is surging.

2009-03-16.abstract: The officers were shot dead in their patrol car in the Jordan Valley area of the West Bank on Sunday night, police officials said.

2009-03-16.title: Fruit Picking Causes Strife in Andalusia as Natives’ Job of Last Resort
2009-03-16.abstract: High unemployment in southern Spain has made fruit picking a lifeline for local people and a source of friction over immigrants.

2009-03-16.abstract: Ukrainians have built a memorial to a scourge of the 1930s. They no longer blame drought for it; they blame Stalin.

2009-03-16.abstract: Some in Iraq are forecasting more troubled times as American forces give up more control to their Iraqi counterparts.

2009-03-17.title: Austria: Pleas Entered in Captivity Case
2009-03-17.abstract: An man who kept his daughter locked up for 24 years and had seven children with her admitted to incest and imprisonment but pleaded not guilty to murder charges.

2009-03-17.abstract: France Bernard d’Espagnat has won the $1.4 million Templeton Prize for his work on the philosophical implications of quantum mechanic.

2009-03-17.title: Vatican: New Language for Web Site
2009-03-17.abstract: The Vatican announced that its Web site would be available in Chinese. It remains to be seen whether China will try to block the site.

2009-03-17.title: Greece: Scotland Yard Sends Officers
2009-03-17.abstract: Britain’s Scotland Yard has sent counterterrorism officers to Greece to advise authorities on how to tackle an upsurge in domestic terrorism and crime in the capital.

2009-03-17.title: Syria: Lebanon Opens Embassy
2009-03-17.abstract: Lebanon opened its embassy in the Syrian capital, the latest step in establishing diplomatic relations between the countries for the first time since they gained independence.

2009-03-18.title: U.S. Weighs Taliban Strike Into Pakistan

2009-03-18.abstract: Andry Rajoelina, a former disc jockey and upstart populist leader, took advantage of a divided military to oust a democratically-elected president and ascend to power.

2009-03-18.abstract: The sharper tone and the appointment of Maj. Gen. J. Scott Gration come after criticism from activists who once saw Barack Obama as an ally in the struggle to save the people of Darfur.

2009-03-18.abstract: With the global economy reeling from the banking crisis, and as the explosive economic growth of China begins to slow, the rise and fall of Pingyao could be read as a cautionary tale.

2009-03-18.title: Bush Gives First Speech Since Leaving Office
2009-03-18.abstract: The speech was closed to reporters, but an attendee said the former president spoke out against trade protectionism in the United States.

2009-03-19.title: U.S. Plans Vastly Expanded Afghan Security Force
2009-03-19.abstract: A costly program would more than double the size of the force in the hope of filling a void left by the central government.

2009-03-19.abstract: Spain’s bullfighters have reacted with uproar over Francisco Rivera Ordóñez’s receipt of a coveted bullfighting prize.

2009-03-19.abstract: In an economy where consumers tend to be less eager shoppers, officials hope the coupons will stimulate buying and avert the worst of an economic downturn.

2009-03-19.abstract: Prosecutors are facing political pressure to avert a fraud and racketeering trial for Jacob Zuma, who is almost certain to become South Africa’s next president after elections scheduled for April 22.

2009-03-19.abstract: An inquiry struck down allegations that an ambassador helped cover up the illegal Chinese origins of ammunition bought by a Pentagon contractor.

2009-03-20.abstract: Sir Nicholas was a gregarious diplomat who labored to win the Reagan administration’s backing for Britain’s military campaign to retake the Falkland Islands.

2009-03-20.abstract: Authoritarian leaders all over the world take steps to create a veneer of democracy. Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi outdoes almost all of them.

2009-03-20.abstract: Three former American secretaries of state and a former secretary of defense met with top Russian officials to mend fences before a meeting between the countries’ presidents.

2009-03-20.abstract: The country has begun to channel silt to fill in low-lying lands prone to flooding and to create new land off its long, exposed coast.

2009-03-20.title: Returning to Zimbabwe’s Classrooms, for Now
2009-03-20.abstract: Opposition leaders in Zimbabwe have made strides in reviving the country’s school system but warn that the progress will be short lived without more aid.

2009-03-21.title: Russian President Welcomes Shift by U.S.
2009-03-21.abstract: Russian President Dmitri A. Medvedev and a group of veteran American policymakers have voiced cautious assertions of optimism about improving relations.

2009-03-21.abstract: For Nir Barkat, the city is Israel’s eternal and indivisible capital: a view in keeping with that of the country’s incoming prime minister, but out of step with the Obama administration.

2009-03-21.title: Travelers, Your Tour Bus Is Boarding for Basra
2009-03-21.abstract: A group of Westerners has taken the first authorized tour of Iraq since 2003. Few problems were reported.

2009-03-21.title: 2 Tied to Colombian Rebels Will Be Extradited to U.S.
2009-03-21.abstract: The arrest of two FARC cocaine brokers, who face charges of conspiring to smuggle the drug from airstrips in Venezuela, may strain U.S.-Venezuela relations.

2009-03-21.abstract: A majority of Security Council members criticized Sudan for expelling foreign aid organizations and closing local ones, exacerbating the humanitarian situation in Darfur.

2009-03-22.title: Pope Tells Clergy in Angola to Work Against Belief in Witchcraft
2009-03-22.abstract: Pope Benedict’s decision to raise the issue of sorcery may please human rights advocates who have reported children suffering abuse as a result of the practice.

2009-03-22.abstract: Iraq will sponsor 500 students who want to study in the United States this year on the condition that they return home to put their knowledge to work.

2009-03-22.title: Manhunt in China for Soldier’s Killer
2009-03-22.abstract: The government news reports said the 18-year-old soldier was attacked while guarding at army barracks in the city of Chongqing on Thursday.

2009-03-22.title: Governor in Russia Is Ousted by Kremlin
2009-03-22.abstract: The dismissed governor, Yuri A. Yevdokimov, is a member of the ruling party, United Russia, but he had been feuding with party leaders lately.

2009-03-22.title: Worries Voiced Over Global Economy
2009-03-22.abstract: The president of the World Bank said the global economy is on pace to shrink by 1 percent to 2 percent this year.

2009-03-23.title: Trade Barriers Rise as Slump Tightens Grip
2009-03-23.abstract: A surge in protectionism is provoking nasty trade disputes and undermining efforts to plot a coordinated response to the global economic downturn.

2009-03-23.title: Sweden Says No to Saving Saab
2009-03-23.abstract: The carmaker has fallen on hard times in a country where Nordic socialism has fallen out of favor.

2009-03-23.title: Fears of More Misery in Darfur
2009-03-23.abstract: President Omar Hassan al-Bashir’s expulsion of aid organizations from Darfur has put the lives of millions of displaced people at risk.

2009-03-23.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI drew the biggest numbers of his African visit, a multitude that a Vatican spokesman put at one million, but was more likely hundreds of thousands.

2009-03-23.abstract: A Federal Express cargo plane burst into flames as it landed outside of Tokyo, and the police confirmed that the pilot and co-pilot had been killed.

2009-03-24.title: China Urges New Money Reserve to Replace Dollar
2009-03-24.abstract: China’s central bank says a currency system controlled by the International Monetary Fund would be more stable and economically viable.

2009-03-24.title: Living in a Sea of Mud, and Drowning in Dread
2009-03-24.abstract: After a devastating hurricane last year, the fear of the next big storm infects a whole town.

2009-03-24.title: Scandals Cloud Succession in Newly Unsure Malaysia
2009-03-24.abstract: Despite being slammed by the global economic downturn, the main worry for the Malaysian government is its dysfunctional political system.

2009-03-24.title: At Least 32 Die in a Wave of Violence Across Iraq
2009-03-24.abstract: At least 19 people were killed by a suicide bomber at the wake for the brother of an official of the Patriotic Union of Kurdistan party.

2009-03-24.title: China: Ceiling Collapse Kills at Least 11 Workers
2009-03-24.abstract: A 33-foot-high ceiling at a chemical storage building in southwestern China collapsed Monday, killing at least 11 workers, according to the state-run news agency.

2009-03-25.title: As Clinton Visits Mexico, Strains Show in Relations
2009-03-25.abstract: Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton will pay a visit Wednesday to an unhappy neighbor that blames America for many of its problems.

2009-03-25.title: U.S. Weighs Sharif as Partner in Pakistan
2009-03-25.abstract: The Obama administration must decide whether Nawaz Sharif is likely to be a reliable ally or an obstructionist force.

2009-03-25.title: Russian Volunteers Keep Eye on Citizens, and the Police
2009-03-25.abstract: A group of Russian lawmakers is pushing to enhance the authority of volunteer police patrols, whose numbers have dwindled since the Soviet collapse.

2009-03-25.title: Global Sports Forum: Formula One Edition
2009-03-25.abstract: At the Global Sports Forum our panels of expert journalists and insiders look at the sprawling world of sports through an international lens. Our topic this time is the upcoming Formula One season, the 60th, which starts in Australia this weekend.

2009-03-25.title: YouTube Blocked in China, Google Says
2009-03-25.abstract: Traffic on the site has dropped to nearly zero, the company said, adding that it didn’t know the reason for the block.

2009-03-26.title: Stimulus Sought for Poorest Countries
2009-03-26.abstract: The U.N. secretary general has suggested that the world’s main economic powers establish a $1 trillion global stimulus package to shore up the economies of the poorest countries.

2009-03-26.title: Kashmir: 17 Militants Die in Clashes
2009-03-26.abstract: The Indian Army said Wednesday that it had killed 17 militants in a five-day battle in Indian-administered Kashmir.

2009-03-26.title: Pakistan: Missile Strikes Kill 11
2009-03-26.abstract: Two missiles fired from an American drone killed seven foreign militants in Makeen, a town in south Waziristan, Pakistani intelligence officials said Wednesday

2009-03-26.title: Russia: Suspect Won’t Run for Mayor
2009-03-26.abstract: Andrei K. Lugovoi, the former K.G.B. agent being sought by British authorities in the radiation killing of a Kremlin critic, will not run for mayor of Sochi.

2009-03-26.abstract: A member of the American team negotiating to remove Radovan Karadzic from power in 1996 said the Bosnian Serb leader was never promised immunity as part of a deal to step down.

2009-03-27.title: Aided by Safety Nets, Europe Resists Stimulus Push
2009-03-27.abstract: Europe says its safety nets automatically provide the spending that the United States Congress has to legislate.

2009-03-27.title: Argentina Decides to Move Up Its Elections
2009-03-27.abstract: By approving the plan to move up congressional elections four months, Argentina's Congress helped the president diminish potential political fallout from a worsening economy.

2009-03-27.title: Somalia: Minister Survives Blast
2009-03-27.abstract: Somalia’s interior minister, Sheik Abdulkadir Ali Omar, survived a land-mine explosion that killed his secretary and two of his bodyguards on Thursday.

2009-03-27.title: Kyrgyzstan: 2 Journalists Attacked
2009-03-27.abstract: The journalists who were beaten late Wednesday in Kyrgyzstan’s capital were the third and fourth journalists to be attacked this month in the former Soviet republic.

2009-03-27.title: China: New Rebukes of Dalai Lama
2009-03-27.abstract: The official English-language newspaper of China published two editorials on Thursday denouncing the Dalai Lama and saying that “any attempt to split Tibet is doomed.”

2009-03-28.title: China: Isotope Lost and Found
2009-03-28.abstract: A lead-encased ball containing hazardous cesium-137 was lost this week during the demolition of a Chinese cement factory, but officials now say it may have been found, the BBC reported.

2009-03-28.title: Kyrgyzstan: Plea for a Fair Election
2009-03-28.abstract: More than 1,000 protesters rallied Friday in Kyrgyzstan’s capital, Bishkek, demanding that a presidential election scheduled for July 23 be fair.

2009-03-28.title: China: YouTube Again Accessible
2009-03-28.abstract: The Chinese government has lifted its block of YouTube, the popular video-sharing Web site run by Google.

2009-03-28.title: Turkey: Crash Kills Politician
2009-03-28.abstract: After two days of searching under heavy snow, Turkish rescue teams reached the wreck of a helicopter, the Interior Ministry announced.

2009-03-28.title: Zimbabwe: Farm Seizures Assailed
2009-03-28.abstract: Prime Minister Morgan Tsvangirai called on Friday for the two ministers who share control over the police to ensure the arrests of all the people illegally seizing white-owned commercial farms.

2009-03-29.title: Haiti Fights to Keep Foreign Aid Flowing
2009-03-29.abstract: In the midst of the global economic turmoil, the U.N. seeks to remind the world's wealthy nations, however embattled their finances, not to forget impoverished nations like Haiti.

2009-03-29.title: Accusations and Penalties Continue to Fly at Melbourne
2009-03-29.abstract: The first race of the season will run under the dark cloud of no one being quite sure whether or not the winner will keep the victory – with the Brawns, Toyotas and Williams cars all facing an appeal court in Paris on 14 April and the wrath of the other teams, because of their rear diffuser. Well, we’ve known that for a few days. But after qualifying the grid got shaken up a little, and a few more accusations flew, and the race has become even more complicated – and that little bit more interesting already.

2009-03-29.title: Post-Race Notes
2009-03-29.abstract: Need I say it? What a race!! Please refer to my blog post below about F1 writing stories that are hard to believe….

2009-03-29.title: Laos and Vietnam: Then and Now
2009-03-29.abstract: A reporter retraced the steps of Antoine Fayard, his great-grandfather, who built and designed roads, dams and canals across colonial Southeast Asia.

2009-03-29.title: Concert for Holocaust Survivors Is Condemned
2009-03-29.abstract: Palestinian activists from the Jenin refugee camp condemned a camp youth orchestra’s performance for Holocaust survivors in Israel.

2009-03-30.title: In Drug War, Mexico Fights Cartel and Itself
2009-03-30.abstract: Mexico has launched a war against drugs but it cannot fully rely on the institutions most needed to carry it out.

2009-03-30.title: Turkey’s Governing Party Wins City Races
2009-03-30.abstract: The party of Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan won a narrow victory in nationwide municipal elections, but registered declines in a number of major cities.

2009-03-30.title: Court Favors Karzai Term Until Election
2009-03-30.abstract: The opinion, which is not binding, is the latest step in a constitutional wrangle between President Hamid Karzai and his political rivals.

2009-03-30.title: Iraqis Snap Up Hummers as Icons of Power
2009-03-30.abstract: Hummers in Baghdad have become symbols of increasing security, returning normality and a yearning for the trappings of sovereignty.

2009-03-30.title: Tiny Island Surrounded by Tension in the Gulf
2009-03-30.abstract: Bahrain has become a flashpoint in a regional conflict after an Iranian official said the desert kingdom was historically a province of Iran.

2009-03-31.title: Sarkozy and Merkel Try to Shape European Unity
2009-03-31.abstract: The French president, Nicolas Sarkozy, and the German chancellor, Angela Merkel, find themselves in a forced marriage in these days of economic crisis.

2009-03-31.title: No Red Cape, or Red Ink, in South Korean Bullrings
2009-03-31.abstract: Some cities have begun promoting bullfighting as a tourist attraction and the government hopes to reignite the passion by legalizing ringside gambling.

2009-03-31.title: Obama Urges Sudan to Allow Aid Groups Back Into the Country
2009-03-31.abstract: President Obama said if Sudan did not back down, he would “find some mechanism” to get supplies to Darfur.

2009-03-31.title: Crafting the Future
2009-03-31.abstract: Designer Dries Van Noten talks about including Indian elements in his pieces.

2009-03-31.title: Local Global Luxury
2009-03-31.abstract: The Emporio Mall in New Delhi is a luxury environment for both Indian and international brands.
